self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can stop stale local approval wording from overruling the stronger live currentHead on advanced Runtime chains.
self_improvement.mechanism: The adopted slice downgrades artifact-local approval wording for stale Runtime approval-boundary artifacts once currentHead has advanced downstream.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP slice, the shared resolver still advertised the old follow-up, record, proof, or capability-boundary approval step even after currentHead had already moved to promotion-readiness.
self_improvement.expected_effect: Operators inspecting stale Runtime boundary artifacts will be redirected to the live currentHead instead of being shown a no-longer-satisfiable local approval step.

decision.rationale: The slice is small, shared, and directly aligns artifact-local wording with currentHead truth without rewriting the integrity model.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
